name: Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos
region_intro: Jogging around Nava Del Barco offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, featuring varied terrain from river valleys along the Tormes River to more challenging mountain paths. Runners can expect a mix of natural trails and routes passing by historical sites like the Castillo de El Barco de Ávila. Best jogging routes around Nava Del Barco The most popular jogging route is Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos, a 14.4 miles (23.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 42 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain within the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes, an easy 3.1 miles (4.9 km) path. This route provides scenic views along the Tormes River and passes by the historic castle. Local runners also love the Running loop from Bohoyo, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with notable elevation changes, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around Nava Del Barco is defined by river valleys, mountain trails, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle riverside paths to more demanding mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Nava Del Barco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Nava Del Barco?
A: There are 19 running routes around Nava Del Barco listed on komoot, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for all types of runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Nava Del Barco?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from gentle river valleys along the Tormes River to more challenging mountain paths within the Sierra de Gredos. You'll find a mix of natural trails and routes passing by historical sites.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Nava Del Barco?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy running routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165074004/castillo-de-el-barco-de-avila-circular-por-alrededores-del-rio-tromes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.9 km path with scenic views along the Tormes River.

Q: What about more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners, there are 7 difficult routes available. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158226849/circos-de-nava-y-de-la-laguna-del-barco-circular-por-la-cuerda-de-riscos-altos-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 23.2 km trail with significant elevation gain in the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Nava Del Barco?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila – Valdecorneja Castle loop from El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.4 km circular route that allows you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What natural sights can I see along the running routes in Nava Del Barco?
A: You can encounter various natural attractions, including the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; waterfall, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; gorge, and serene lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical landmarks to explore while running?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass by historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecorneja Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark, and the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4516184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; itself offers historical charm.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails?
A: Yes, for breathtaking vistas, consider routes that lead to or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4297239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Puerto de Tornavacas&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Nava Del Barco?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with mild temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter in the Sierra de Gredos can bring colder temperatures and snow at higher altitudes, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are running routes in Nava Del Barco generally dog-friendly?
A: Most natural trails in the Nava Del Barco area are dog-friendly, especially those outside protected areas. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or in areas with wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the easier, flatter routes, particularly those along the Tormes River, are well-suited for families. These paths often have less elevation gain and provide enjoyable scenery for all ages.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Nava Del Barco?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse landscape, which combines serene river valleys with the dramatic beauty of the Sierra de Gredos. The mix of natural trails and historical landmarks also adds a unique appeal to the running experience.
tips: The hidden Laguna de la Nava is hard to come by. The trail goes up a cobbled path and finally the artificial wall appears, built to increase the capacity of the original bucket excavated by the old glaciers that modeled the Corral del Diablo, the energetic and dark amphitheater that surrounds the rounded lagoon. The PR-AV 39 trail ends at the water's edge. The trail route continues along the path that goes up the eastern slope of the glacial cirque, the best place to continue running up the mountain. The path passes through some ruins and climbs towards the valley of the Ronchito stream.

The Laguna de los Caballeros is in a beautiful glacial cirque in the western sector of the Sierra de Gredos at an altitude of 2,000 meters, surrounded by slopes shaped by ancient glaciations and granite peaks that seem like untouchable sentinels of eternal stones.

The descent to the Barco lagoon follows a rough and delicate terrain through the Cuadrada lagoon to the shore of the great mountain lake where the Galín Gómez gorge begins. On the wall of the lagoon there is a mountain refuge and the short-distance path PR-AV 36 begins, which descends to the right of the glacial basin. The layout of the path improves immediately, especially when it reaches the terrain of the mountain pastures. At the Chozo de Anselmo de Las Escaleruelas you have to leave the short-distance trail, which follows the main lane to the left, and continue straight down towards the valley basin along a hillside complicated by vegetation. The path first passes over two ditches and a couple of bridges and then crosses the main stream of the valley, descending from the high peaks along the right bank of the gorge. The layout is imprecise at first and sometimes they get lost in the grassy fields.
